06 QSP  Quarterly Sprint Planning - YouTube.mp4
How do you eat an elephant? Well, the answer, of course, is one bite at a time. Now, I freely admit that this expression is a little bit trite and kind of outright disgusting if you think too long. About it. But the lesson is clear. When you're faced with an overwhelming objective, the best way to tackle it is one step, or in this case, one bite at a time. While we have narrowed our scope from the five and ten-year targets that, you know, most consultants and business books have you set down to a more manageable three-year cycle, three years. For most bootstrapped entrepreneurial companies, is still a fairly long time. So if our three-year target is the elephant, then our quarterly planning cycles— are the bites.
If you recall from the previous lesson, we use a tool called the 12Q planning grid to break our three-year targets into 12 manageable 90-day chunks of time. And it's these 12 quarterly sprints that will form the basis of your company's meeting rhythm. Here's how it works. Every 90 days, you and your leadership team will hold a one to two-day quarterly sprint planning meeting. The goal of these meetings is to create your quarterly sprint plan or QSP for short— for the next quarter. Now we'll cover quarterly sprint planning in another lesson, but here are the basic steps to hosting a quarterly sprint planning meeting and building your QSP. Step one, review your company's financials and determine if you are on track or behind on your three-year target. 12 Q plan.
Step two, identify the scorecard metrics that are most worthy of your team's focus for the next 90 days. You know, the ones that must be improved and they must be up. If you're going to hit or ideally exceed your growth goals for that quarter. These are what we refer to as your North Star metrics. Step three, brainstorm. and green light— the key initiatives your team will execute over the next 90 days to improve those North Star metrics. Step four: select a theme or route. crying for the quarter that keeps your team focused and motivated. And finally, step five: review your company's operating system and make any necessary adjustments and upgrades. To your company scorecards, high output, team canvas, playbooks, value, engines, and clarity.
Compass now, quarterly sprint planning meetings are typically held either on the last or the second last week in a given quarter, because by then you usually have a pretty good sense of how that particular quarter is going to end and the steps that need to be taken to close any gaps in your 12Q plan. And while it's not the end of the world if you have to wait until the first week in the quarter to hold your quarterly sprint planning meeting, it's generally better to plan too early the previous quarter than too late in the current quarter. Now, as a side note, while I realize it isn't always possible for teams to gather in person, this is one meeting where I would absolutely do everything you can to get everyone on your leadership team in the same room.
Quarterly sprint planning can happen virtually, but it is far... less effective and, frankly, far more exhausting. So, if you must hold a virtual QSP meeting, my suggestion is you do two to three half days rather than one. Two full days, because let's face it, after a few hours on Zoom, even the most remote-friendly teams can become fatigued. And this is one meeting where you really need to stay sharp. Now, in addition to the quarterly sprint planning meetings, your quarterly meeting rhythm should also include a company-wide all-hands meeting where you announced the results of the previous quarter and roll out the new theme, success metrics, and key initiatives for the upcoming quarter. Now, if you're already holding monthly or even weekly company all-hands meetings, then you can incorporate the QSP announcements into the already scheduled meeting slots. Either way, if you schedule a two-day quarterly sprint planning. at the end of every quarter and a company-wide all-hands meeting the following week, then you can check the box on your quarterly meeting rhythm. Now, let's talk about the monthly meeting rhythm.
